text.title: creational-patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for creational-patterns. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Default to Dependency Injection for any component with dependencies. Never reach for Singleton unless the dependency is truly process-global, read-only, and stateless.
rule.rationale: DI reduces maintenance bugs by ~27%, improves testability (70% of high-performing teams rely on mocking), enables modularity, and accelerates refactoring. DI frameworks exist in every mainstream language.

rule.statement: Use Builder when a constructor has more than 3 parameters or any optional parameter, instead of overloading constructors or passing positional `None`/`null` placeholders.
rule.rationale: Telescoping constructors are unreadable and break call-site refactoring. Builders make required vs optional explicit and produce immutable results.

rule.statement: Use Factory Method when the concrete subclass to instantiate depends on runtime input (config, env, request payload). Do not litter `if/elif` constructor switches across business logic.
rule.rationale: Centralizing the switch in one factory keeps domain code closed against new variants and makes it possible to add a new variant by registering it once.
